steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens by noting that feathered dinosaurs are commonly associated with wings, then uses 'However' to pivot to its actual main point: many non-winged dinosaurs also had feathers, illustrated by Yutyrannus. The question asks for the choice that best describes the passage's main purpose.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To discuss the presence of feathers on certain types of dinosaurs: matches the passage's central contrast between the common assumption (wings = feathers) and the reality (many feathered dinosaurs lacked wings). ✓
  • Choice B — To point out the differences between dinosaur feathers and bird feathers: the passage never compares bird feathers to dinosaur feathers — this topic is absent. ✗
  • Choice C — To explain why feathered dinosaurs went extinct: extinction is not discussed anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — To argue that only one species of dinosaur had feathers: the passage presents Yutyrannus as one example among many, not the sole feathered dinosaur. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
